description: On a short hike and at activity stations, discover the wild side of the lake, hills, and valleys in the park. Learn about wildlife, how humans are impacting their habitats, and how we can be good neighbors. Topics will include ecosystems, urbanization, animal adaptations, and conservation.

description: Using the scientific method, field research and instruments, students will discover a riparian ecosystem while becoming Streamside Scientists. A short Naturalist led walk will take us to the Arroyo Valle stream to rotate through activity stations. *Programs held at Arroyo Staging Area on Arroyo Rd.*

city: Livermore, Pleasanton, Dublin, San Ramon, Danville
description: Who did that doo-doo? Scat tells an important story of which animals have visited an area, and sometimes their doo-doo helps us make inferences about the health of the ecosystem community. Let's become "grossologists" by creating models of animal scat while our classmates identify who did that doo-doo.

program_name: The Story of Del Valle: Honoring Native Peoples, Ranching History, and Lakeside Fun
city: Livermore, Pleasanton, Dublin, San Ramon, Danville
description: Come along on a journey through time as we explore the history of Del Valle Regional Park! We'll learn about the Taunan Ohlone people, the first caretakers of this land, and how ranching shaped the valley years later. We'll see how the lake was built, how Del Valle became the fun place we visit today, and how people and nature together continue to shape its future.
